title: astragalus graecus
intro: Astragalus graecus, also known as the Greek milk-vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Astragalus graecus are numerous, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-viral properties. Astragalus graecus can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
